rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 13, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. All proceedings stayed per October 29, 2024 Court of Appeal Order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ADAM FAGEL ET AL VS. OYVIND SEAN KINSEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 13, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT OYVIND KINSEY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Parties are ordered to engage in meaningful good faith meet and confer efforts in person or over the phone on a mutually agreeable date between November 14, 2024 and November 22, 2024. If parties cannot agree on a date, parties much meet and confer at 2:00 p.m. on November 20, 2024. No later than November 27, 2024 parties to file declarations attesting to good faith participation in a meet and confer conference. Thereafter, if necessary, Defendants may re-file the demurrer no later than December 6, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE VINCENT TOWNSEND JR. ET AL VS. MELVIN WASHINGTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 13, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF CLARENCE TOWNSEND , AS TRUSTEE OF THE TOWNSEND LIVING TRUST DATED AUGUST 2, 2022, JULIE TOWNSEND, CLARENCE TOWNSEND, LAURA TOWNSEND Motion For Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry their burden re: probability of prevailing on the merits and hardship as to the claims for easement over "virtually the entire square footage of" Defendant's lot for encroachment, gardening, landscaping and social gatherings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 1499 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. ANDREI MIKHAILOV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 13, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF 1499 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Hearing Required to address the exact amount currently owed. Late opposition, served by regular mail filed in violation of CCP 1005 and 1013, is stricken. The Court further notes that this late opposition was filed by Cathy Mosbrucker, not an attorney of record and did not limited scope counsel for this hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
